documents.title: Plastic Bottles (#1 PET)
documents.instructions: Empty, rinse, and place in the gray recycling cart. Caps and labels can stay on.
documents.notes: Berkeley accepts all rigid plastics #1-#7 in the recycling cart.

documents.title: Aluminum Cans
documents.instructions: Empty and rinse. Place in gray recycling cart.
documents.notes: Crushing is optional but saves space.

documents.title: Cardboard & Paperboard
documents.instructions: Flatten boxes. Remove tape and plastic liners. Place in recycling cart.
documents.notes: Greasy pizza boxes go in the green compost cart, not recycling.

documents.title: Glass Bottles & Jars
documents.instructions: Empty and rinse. Remove lids. Place in gray recycling cart.
documents.notes: No window glass, mirrors, or ceramics.

documents.title: Food Scraps & Compostables
documents.instructions: Place in the green compost cart. Use a compostable bag or paper liner.
documents.notes: Berkeley has mandatory composting for food waste.

documents.title: Styrofoam / Expanded Polystyrene
documents.instructions: Not accepted in curbside recycling. Place in gray landfill cart or take to special drop-off.
documents.notes: Check Berkeley's Zero Waste website for foam drop-off events.

documents.title: Plastic Bags & Film
documents.instructions: Do NOT place in curbside recycling. Take clean bags to a store drop-off bin.
documents.notes: Bags jam recycling machinery at the MRF.

documents.title: Batteries
documents.instructions: Never place in any curbside bin. Take to Berkeley's Hazardous Waste facility or a retailer drop-off.
documents.notes: Lithium batteries are a fire hazard in trucks.

documents.title: Electronics & E-Waste
documents.instructions: Take to Berkeley's Transfer Station or an approved e-waste collection event.
